Title: Metabolic GWAS-based dissection of genetic basis underlying nutrient quality variation and domestication of cassava storage root

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-sugar donor to the 3-O position of flavonols/anthocyanidins to form 3-O-glucosides.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for modification/glycosylation of flavonol-derived terminal metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant-related flavonol derivative associated with nutritional quality
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Storage root; also measured in leaf after silencing
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher transcript abundance in leaf and stem than in storage root
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in leaf and stem than in storage root; profiled across seven storage-root developmental stages
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mGWAS; in vitro enzyme assay; transient virus-induced gene silencing; qRT-PCR; allele comparison; site-directed mutagenesis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics (MetaboLights: MTBLS9027); whole-genome resequencing (NCBI BioProject: PRJNA578024); RNA-seq (NCBI BioProject: PRJNA578024)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to enhance beneficial flavonol glycosides in cassava roots through allele selection or engineering
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Me3GT was identified by mGWAS as the candidate gene controlling 8-C-hexosyl-kaempferol O-hexoside variation. 2. Recombinant Me3GT showed 3-O-glucosyltransferase activity toward kaempferol, quercetin, pelargonidin, and cyanidin but not tested 5-O/7-O substrates. 3. Silencing Me3GT reduced the target metabolite level. 4. Multiple nonsynonymous SNPs, especially P334S and others, altered enzyme activity and metabolite accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Lead SNP 8:38593261; likely causative coding SNP 8:38681061 (P334S). Coding-region SNPs formed two alleles with different enzymatic activity and metabolite levels.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Glycosylates acetone cyanohydrin using UDP-glucose to produce linamarin.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-related metabolite that is toxic to humans and negatively affects cassava edibility
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Storage root; also measured in leaf after silencing
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly co-expressed with CYP79D1 and CYP71E7b; expression examined in storage root and inducible contexts
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly co-expressed with CYP79D1 and CYP71E7b in storage root; induced context not clearly stated for UGT85K5, whereas UGT85K4 responded to MeJA/Xam
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: CG-pathway genes reported as responsive to MeJA and Xanthomonas axonopodis pv. manihotis infection at the cluster level
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes linamarin accumulation; allelic variation contributes to low-linamarin haplotypes
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key terminal biosynthetic enzyme for linamarin formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mGWAS; in vitro enzyme assay; transient gene silencing; qRT-PCR; co-expression analysis; haplotype/allele association
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics (MetaboLights: MTBLS9027); whole-genome resequencing (NCBI BioProject: PRJNA578024); RNA-seq (NCBI BioProject: PRJNA578024)
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reducing linamarin may affect defense-associated cyanogenic glucoside function, though identified haplotypes were not significantly associated with cassava bacterial blight resistance in this study
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for breeding low-cyanogenic cassava by selecting favorable allele combinations with CYP79D1 and CYP71E7b
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT85K5 catalyzed formation of linamarin from acetone cyanohydrin in vitro. 2. It was more strongly co-expressed with CYP79D1 and CYP71E7b than UGT85K4. 3. Silencing UGT85K5 significantly reduced linamarin. 4. A 3'UTR/downstream-associated SNP and allele combinations with CYP79D1/CYP71E7b contributed to low linamarin content.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Associated SNP Chr12:35953769 near/downstream of UGT85K5; three 3'UTR SNPs significantly associated with linamarin. Paper emphasizes UGT85K5 over UGT85K4 as the major causal member in the cluster.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts amino acid precursors such as valine/isoleucine into aldoxime intermediates for cyanogenic glucoside biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-related but detrimental to food safety at high levels
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly co-expressed with CYP71E7b and UGT85K5 in storage root; induced by MeJA at 6 h or 12 h and by Xam infection at 7 dpi
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly co-expressed with CYP71E7b and UGT85K5 in storage root; stress inducible under MeJA and Xam infection
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA treatment; Xanthomonas axonopodis pv. manihotis infection
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Allelic variation is associated with lower linamarin content
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key upstream pathway enzyme; rate-limiting not established in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mGWAS; co-expression analysis; SNP association; haplotype combination analysis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower cyanogenic glucosides may alter defense, although haplotypes were not significantly associated with cassava bacterial blight resistance here
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Part of favorable low-linamarin allele combination for breeding safer cassava
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP79D1 resides in the cyanogenic-glucoside biosynthetic cluster associated with linamarin. 2. It is highly co-expressed with CYP71E7b and UGT85K5. 3. A 3'UTR SNP (Chr12:35990792) is associated with lower linamarin. 4. Combined favorable alleles with CYP71E7b and UGT85K5 produced the lowest linamarin content.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter SNPs were associated with linamarin but expression of CYP79D1 was not significantly correlated with linamarin across 22 cultivars, weakening causality of those promoter variants.

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Cyanogenic-pathway cytochrome P450 of the CYP71 family
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Downstream cyanohydrin intermediate en route to linamarin
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes downstream oxidation/rearrangement steps converting CYP79-derived oxime intermediates toward cyanohydrin formation in cyanogenic glucoside biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms intermediate en route to target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly co-expressed with CYP79D1 and UGT85K5 in storage root; induced by MeJA and Xam infection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly co-expressed with CYP79D1 and UGT85K5 in storage root; induced by MeJA and Xam infection
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA treatment; Xanthomonas axonopodis pv. manihotis infection
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Allelic variation contributes to linamarin-level differences and favorable combined haplotypes with low linamarin
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key midstream enzyme; not established as rate limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mGWAS; co-expression analysis; SNP association; haplotype combination analysis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential defense tradeoff if cyanogenic content is reduced, but no significant association with bacterial blight resistance haplotypes was found
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful in allele stacking with CYP79D1 and UGT85K5 for low-linamarin cassava improvement
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71E7b lies within the CG biosynthesis gene cluster associated with linamarin. 2. It is highly co-expressed with CYP79D1 and UGT85K5. 3. An intergenic SNP near CYP71E7b is associated with linamarin content. 4. Its favorable allele contributes to the lowest-linamarin allelic combination.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No significant associations were observed between resequenced SNPs within CYP71E7b and linamarin in follow-up fine-mapping, so support is weaker than for CYP79D1/UGT85K5.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts dihydroflavonols into flavonols in the flavonoid pathway, contributing to quercetin-derived metabolite production.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonol linked to nutritional quality but negatively associated with storage-root weight per plant in this study
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression in storage root significantly correlated with quercetin 3-O-glucoside among 22 cultivars
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in storage root; variation correlated with quercetin 3-O-glucoside content
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes quercetin 3-O-glucoside accumulation and is negatively associated with storage-root weight per plant
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch enzyme for flavonol production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mGWAS/pGWAS co-localization; expression-metabolite correlation; allele association; dual-luciferase promoter assay; population genetics/selection analysis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics (MTBLS9027); whole-genome resequencing (PRJNA578024; PRJNA234389 for additional wild data); RNA-seq (PRJNA578024)
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher MeFLS1 activity/higher quercetin 3-O-glucoside is associated with lower storage-root weight per plant
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent target for breeding high-yield cassava with reduced quercetin 3-O-glucoside through selection/editing of favorable low-expression alleles
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MeFLS1 was identified at a locus co-associated with quercetin 3-O-glucoside and storage-root weight per plant. 2. MeFLS1 expression positively correlated with quercetin 3-O-glucoside. 3. A promoter-region variant reduced promoter activity, expression, and quercetin 3-O-glucoside while marginally increasing yield. 4. The locus showed signatures of domestication selection in cultivated cassava.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper text inconsistently describes the causative SNP position as promoter/3'UTR, but functional dual-luciferase evidence supports promoter-effect interpretation for SNP 2:12776464.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB transcription factor; ortholog of AtMYB4
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of anthocyanin biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates/promotes expression of MeCHI and MeF3H
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Allelic variation in 5'UTR/promoter affecting promoter activity
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: MYB genes are discussed as components of MYB-bHLH-WDR complexes generally; direct complex involvement for MeMYB4 was not experimentally shown here
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigmentation and nutritional antioxidant quality; determines red storage-root endothelium
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression profiled in leaf, root, stem, and across seven storage-root developmental stages; expression in storage root correlated with anthocyanin levels among 22 cultivars
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf, root, stem, and storage-root developmental stages; positively correlated with cyanidin 3-O-glucoside and delphinidin 3-O-rutinoside in storage root
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes cyanidin 3-O-glucoside and delphinidin 3-O-rutinoside accumulation and red storage-root endothelium color
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive regulator underlying natural variation in anthocyanin content and endothelium color in cassava storage root
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mGWAS/pGWAS co-localization; promoter allele dual-luciferase assay; expression-metabolite correlation; transient silencing (VIGS); qRT-PCR; yeast one-hybrid
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pTRV-based VIGS for silencing; native promoter alleles tested by dual luciferase
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for breeding cassava with desirable red endothelium and elevated anthocyanin nutrition by selecting favorable high-activity alleles
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MeMYB4 was identified by co-localized metabolic and phenotypic GWAS for anthocyanins and root endothelium color. 2. A 5'UTR/promoter SNP altered promoter activity and was associated with anthocyanin levels and red coloration. 3. Silencing MeMYB4 reduced cyanidin 3-O-glucoside, delphinidin 3-O-rutinoside, and expression of MeCHI/MeF3H. 4. MeMYB4 directly binds promoters of MeCHI and MeF3H, explaining its positive regulation of anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although MYB4 proteins are often repressors of phenylpropanoid metabolism, MeMYB4 behaved as a positive regulator in cassava anthocyanin accumulation.
